description: Flexible event logging system for applications and libraries.

functions.name: getLogger

params.name: name
params.description: Logger name, typically a dot-separated hierarchical name. If omitted, returns the root logger.

returns.description: A logger with the specified name, creating it if necessary.

functions.description: Return a logger with the specified name, creating it if necessary. If no name is specified, return the root logger.

functions.name: basicConfig

params.name: filename
params.description: Use a FileHandler with the specified filename rather than a StreamHandler.

params.name: filemode
params.description: File mode for filename. Defaults to 'a'.

params.name: format
params.description: Format string for the handler. Defaults to '%(levelname)s:%(name)s:%(message)s'.

params.name: datefmt
params.description: Date/time format string for asctime in the format string.

params.name: level
params.description: Set the root logger level to the specified level.

params.name: stream
params.description: Use the specified stream to initialize the StreamHandler. Incompatible with filename.

functions.description: Do basic configuration for the logging system. Does nothing if the root logger already has handlers configured.

functions.name: debug

params.name: msg
params.description: The message format string.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments merged into msg using string formatting.

params.name: exc_info
params.description: If true, exception information is added to the message.

functions.description: Log a message with level DEBUG on the root logger.

params.name: msg

params.description: If true, exception information is added to the message.

params.name: msg

functions.name: critical

params.name: msg
params.description: The message format string.

params.name: args

params.description: If true, exception information is added to the message.

functions.name: exception

params.name: msg
params.description: The message format string.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments merged into msg using string formatting.

functions.description: Log a message with level ERROR on the root logger, with exception information. Should only be called from an exception handler.

functions.name: log

params.name: level
params.description: The numeric logging level.

params.name: msg
params.description: The message format string.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments merged into msg using string formatting.

params.name: exc_info
params.description: If true, exception information is added to the message.

functions.description: Log a message with the given level on the root logger.

functions.name: disable

params.name: level
params.description: All logging calls at this level and below will be disabled.

functions.description: Provides an overriding level for all loggers which takes precedence over the logger's own level.

functions.name: addLevelName

params.name: level
params.description: The numeric level value.

params.name: levelName
params.description: The text name to associate with the level.

functions.description: Associate levelName with level. Used to define custom levels.

functions.name: getLevelName

params.name: level
params.description: The numeric logging level.

returns.description: The text representation of the logging level.

functions.description: Return the textual representation of a logging level. If the level is not found, returns 'Level %s' % level.

functions.name: makeLogRecord

params.name: attrdict
params.description: Dictionary of attributes to set on the LogRecord.

returns.description: A new LogRecord whose attributes are defined by the dictionary.

functions.description: Create a new LogRecord with attributes defined by the given dictionary. Useful for reconstituting a LogRecord from a pickled event.

functions.name: setLoggerClass

params.name: klass
params.description: A subclass of Logger to be used when instantiating new loggers.

functions.description: Set the class to be used when instantiating a logger. The class should define __init__() such that only a name argument is required.

classes.name: Logger
classes.description: Loggers are never instantiated directly; always use logging.getLogger(name). A logger represents a single logging channel.

params.name: level
params.description: The numeric logging level.

params.name: args
params.description: Arguments merged into msg using string formatting.

params.name: exc_info
params.description: If true, exception information is added to the message.

methods.description: Log a message with the given integer level on this logger.

methods.name: setLevel

params.name: level
params.description: The logging level, as an integer or string name (e.g. 'INFO').

methods.description: Set the logging level of this logger. Messages below this level will be ignored.

methods.name: addHandler

params.name: hdlr
params.description: A handler to add to this logger.

methods.description: Add the specified handler to this logger.

methods.name: removeHandler

params.name: hdlr
params.description: A handler to remove from this logger.

methods.description: Remove the specified handler from this logger.

methods.name: addFilter

params.name: filt
params.description: A filter to add to this logger.

methods.description: Add the specified filter to this logger.

methods.name: removeFilter

params.name: filt
params.description: A filter to remove from this logger.

methods.description: Remove the specified filter from this logger.

methods.name: isEnabledFor

params.name: level
params.description: The numeric logging level to check.

returns.description: True if a message at the given level would be processed by this logger.

methods.description: Check if this logger is enabled for the given level.

methods.name: getEffectiveLevel
methods.description: Return the effective level for this logger. If a level has been set, return it. Otherwise, traverse to the parent until a level is found.

methods.name: getChild

params.name: suffix
params.description: The suffix to append to this logger's name.

methods.description: Return a logger which is a descendant of this logger, determined by the suffix.

methods.name: hasHandlers

returns.description: True if a handler was found.

methods.description: Check if this logger has any handlers configured. Looks up the logger hierarchy.

fields.name: propagate
fields.description: If true, events are passed to handlers of higher level loggers. Defaults to True.

fields.name: handlers
fields.description: The list of handlers directly attached to this logger instance.

fields.name: level
fields.description: The logging level set for this logger.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: The parent logger in the hierarchy.

classes.name: Handler
classes.description: Base class for handlers. Handlers send log records to the appropriate destination. Never instantiate directly; use subclasses.

params.name: level
params.description: Initial logging level. Defaults to NOTSET (all messages processed).

constructors.description: Initialize the handler with an optional level.

methods.name: emit

params.name: record
params.description: The log record to emit.

methods.description: Do whatever it takes to actually log the specified record. Subclasses must implement this.

methods.name: handle

params.name: record
params.description: The log record to handle.

methods.description: Conditionally emit the specified record, depending on filters which may have been added.

methods.name: setFormatter

params.name: fmt
params.description: The formatter to use for this handler.

methods.description: Set the Formatter for this handler.

methods.name: addFilter

params.name: filt
params.description: A filter to add.

methods.description: Add the specified filter to this handler.

methods.name: removeFilter

params.name: filt
params.description: A filter to remove.

methods.description: Remove the specified filter from this handler.

methods.name: setLevel

params.name: level
params.description: The logging level.

methods.description: Set the logging level of this handler. Records below this level are ignored.

methods.name: close
methods.description: Tidy up any resources used by the handler.

methods.name: flush
methods.description: Ensure all logging output has been flushed.

classes.name: Formatter
classes.description: Responsible for converting a LogRecord to an output string to be interpreted by a human or external system.

params.name: fmt
params.description: A format string using LogRecord attributes (e.g. '%(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s'). Defaults to raw message.

params.name: datefmt
params.description: A date/time format string for time.strftime(). Defaults to ISO8601.

constructors.description: Initialize the formatter with optional format and date format strings.

methods.name: format

params.name: record
params.description: The log record to format.

methods.description: Format the specified record as text.

methods.name: formatTime

params.name: record
params.description: The log record.

params.name: datefmt
params.description: Optional date format string override.

methods.description: Format the creation time of the record.

methods.name: formatException

params.name: exc_info
params.description: Exception tuple (type, value, traceback).

methods.description: Format the specified exception information as a string.

classes.name: StreamHandler
classes.description: A handler that writes logging output to a stream such as sys.stdout or sys.stderr.

params.name: stream
params.description: The output stream. Defaults to sys.stderr.

constructors.description: Initialize the handler with the given stream.

methods.name: emit

params.name: record
params.description: The log record to emit.

methods.description: Write the formatted record to the stream.

methods.name: flush
methods.description: Flush the stream.

classes.name: FileHandler
classes.description: A handler that writes formatted logging records to disk files.

params.name: filename
params.description: Path to the log file.

params.name: mode
params.description: File open mode. Defaults to 'a' (append).

params.name: encoding
params.description: File encoding. If None, the default encoding is used.

params.name: delay
params.description: If true, file opening is deferred until the first emit() call.

constructors.description: Open the specified file and use it as the stream for logging.

methods.name: emit

params.name: record
params.description: The log record to emit.

methods.description: Write the formatted record to the file.

methods.name: close
methods.description: Close the file.

classes.name: NullHandler
classes.description: A no-op handler. Used by library authors to prevent 'No handler found' warnings. Available since Python 2.7.

methods.name: emit

params.name: record
params.description: The log record (ignored).

methods.description: This method does nothing.

classes.name: Filter
classes.description: A filter that allows only records from a specific logger and its children to pass through.

params.name: name
params.description: Logger name prefix. If empty, all records are allowed.

constructors.description: Initialize with the name of the logger which, together with its children, will have its events allowed through.

methods.name: filter

params.name: record
params.description: The log record to check.

returns.description: True if the record should be logged, False otherwise.

methods.description: Determine if the specified record should be logged. Returns True if the record should be processed.

constants.name: DEBUG
constants.description: Numeric level 10. Detailed information, typically of interest only when diagnosing problems.

constants.name: INFO
constants.description: Numeric level 20. Confirmation that things are working as expected.

constants.name: WARNING
constants.description: Numeric level 30. An indication that something unexpected happened or may happen.

constants.name: ERROR
constants.description: Numeric level 40. Due to a more serious problem, the software has not been able to perform some function.

constants.name: CRITICAL
constants.description: Numeric level 50. A serious error indicating the program may be unable to continue running.

constants.name: NOTSET
constants.description: Numeric level 0. When set on a logger, the parent's level is used to determine effective level.
